title: Workspace Model spec — implementation
Subsystem envelope declaration (S06)
Minimum git version pin (≥ 2.34) detected at startup
Workspace type and required fields
Worktree path convention `<repo>/.harmonik/worktrees/<run_id>/`
Worktree creation uses `git worktree add -b <branch> <path> <parent_commit>`
Discovery classifies partially-crashed worktree states
Workspace ID derivable from run_id (`workspace_id = "ws-" + run_id`)
Task branch named `run/<run_id>`
Sub-workflow expansion does not create additional task branches
Integration branch naming convention (default + parent-bead-derived)
Ref-safe bead-ID substitution via `git check-ref-format`
Three-level branching model (task → integration → main)
Small-scope collapse: operator-policy-gated merge target for parentless runs
Branch naming is stable across a harmonik minor version
Lease is held by the run, not by individual agents
One active agent at a time inside a workspace
One run per bead at a time
Workspace ID discoverable from run_id without a separate index
Lease-lock file canonical path, JSON content, atomic-write + fsync birth
Lease release on terminal transitions; per-terminal-path durability gates
Lease discovery on startup (filesystem walk + lock-content read)
Released-workspace path re-use is forbidden
`.gitignore` hygiene for harmonik control-plane paths (write-or-fail)
Workspace state machine
Workspace lifecycle event emission obligations (WHEN)
`workspace_leased` emitted after sidecar + lease-lock fsync (4-step ordering)
Merge-back is performed by a node in the same worktree (same lease)
Merge-back node dispatch contract (non-agentic OR agentic)
Squash-merge with `--strategy=ort`; one-commit-per-task; trailers + author/committer split
Merge executes outside main worktree (`--ignore-other-worktrees` OR scratch worktree)
Squash-merge is non-fast-forward by construction
Merge outcome emits workspace_merge_status with status=merged
Original-implementer identification: sidecar walk (NOT trailer walk)
All-mechanical task branches escalate directly (skip re-dispatch)
Unresolvable conflicts escalate via merge_conflict_escalation
Conflict-resolver dispatch (mechanism) + 3-attempt cap (op-tunable [1,10])
Session-log directory at `${workspace_path}/.harmonik/sessions/${session_id}/`
Metadata sidecar `harmonik.meta.json` (atomic write: tmp+fsync+rename+parent-fsync)
Sidecar metadata stamping precedes workspace_leased for the first session
bead_id propagates into session metadata when present
Session-log directory consumed read-only by S08
Post-merge session-log retention default (preserve-in-merged-branch)
Failed-run worktrees persist until operator cleanup
Failed-run workspace state is `discarded`; interrupt_state composes orthogonally
Startup orphan sweep removes stale lease-lock files (content-first staleness; mtime tiebreaker)
reopen-bead → fresh run_id + fresh worktree + fresh task branch
Intra-run rollback verdicts keep the same worktree + task branch
Re-run vs intra-run classification deterministic on 7-value verdict enum
interrupt_state orthogonal to lifecycle for in-flight states (5-value enum)
Terminal lifecycle states carry no interrupt signal
interrupt_state set by operator + reconciliation pathways; WM is sole writer
Workspace-local marker for operator/verdict-driven interrupt_state mutations
WM observes interrupt transitions but does NOT emit workspace_interrupted
interrupt_state reset to `none` requires reconciliation OR operator resume
Sensor: lease-by-run is honored corpus-wide
Sensor: one in-flight run per bead
Sensor: checkpoint commits obey git append-only on the task branch (two-part)
Sensor: canonical-path discovery without a registry
Define `Workspace` record (§6.1)
Define `LeaseLockFile` record (§6.1)
Define `WorkspaceState` enum (§6.1)
Define `InterruptState` enum (§6.1)
Define `SessionMetadataSidecar` record (§6.1)
WM error taxonomy (§8) — 12-class typed sentinel set + transitions + downstream routing
Worktree primitive + crash-evidence fixture (WM-001..004 + WM-003a)
Branch-naming + ref-safe substitution fixture (WM-005..009 + WM-005a + WM-006a)
Lease-lifecycle + crash-recovery fixture (WM-010..013 + WM-013a..e)
Merge-back + scratch-worktree fixture (WM-018..021 + WM-018a + WM-019a)
Conflict-resolution + 3-attempt-cap harness (WM-022..024 + WM-022a)
Session-log + atomic sidecar fixture (WM-025..030)
Failed-run + verdict + interrupt-state harness (WM-031..036 + WM-037..040 + WM-037a + WM-038a + WM-INV-003 part B)
